executiveSummary.text: Comcast presents as a deep value trap or a genuine bargain depending on your prior view of the broadband cord-cutting thesis. At 5.5x earnings, the market is pricing that the earnings decline is structural and permanent. The P/E of 5.5x and dividend yield of 3.8% are facts in evidence of that pessimism. The question is whether the bear case is too pessimistic. Q4 results showed mixed trends: broadband subscriber losses continuing but partially offset by Xfinity Mobile subscriber growth and NBCUniversal content revenues. Peacock streaming reached 36 million subscribers. The convergence strategy — bundling broadband, streaming, and mobile — is the bull thesis. Structural bear: cable cord-cutting has been underway for a decade and there is no evidence it has stopped. SPECULATIVE BUY reflects the valuation support at 5.5x but not a high-conviction view given the secular headwinds.
